title: Five Tribes
aliases: Five Tribes: The Djinns of Naqala

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Tiles
- **Sultan tiles** — 30 double-sided tiles forming a 5x6 grid (the Sultanate of Naqala).
- **Tile types** — Oases (blue), Villages (red), Markets (small & large), Sacred Places (orange), and generic tiles with various point values (5-15 VP).

#### Meeples (90 total)
- **Viziers (yellow)** — 16 meeples.
- **Elders (white)** — 20 meeples.
- **Merchants (green)** — 18 meeples.
- **Builders (blue)** — 18 meeples.
- **Assassins (red)** — 18 meeples.

#### Cards & Tokens
- **Djinn cards** — 22 cards with special powers.
- **Resource cards** — 54 cards (Ivory, Jewels, Gold, Papyrus, Silk, Spices, Fish, Wheat, Pottery).
- **Palm trees** — Wooden palm tokens.
- **Palaces** — Wooden palace tokens.
- **Coins** — Gold coins (1 and 5 denominations).
- **Turn order / bid track** — Track for bidding on turn order.
- **Camels** — 8 per player (claim markers for tiles).

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Grid Setup
1. **Shuffle tiles** — Shuffle all 30 Sultan tiles face down.
2. **Place grid** — Lay tiles face up in a 5x6 grid.
3. **Populate meeples** — Place all 90 meeples in a bag, then randomly draw and place 3 meeples on each of the 30 tiles.
4. **Resource deck** — Shuffle the 54 resource cards and place face down. Reveal the top 9 cards in a face-up row (the market).
5. **Djinn deck** — Shuffle the 22 Djinn cards and place face down. Reveal the top 3 face up.
6. **Palm trees and palaces** — Place in supply piles near the board.
7. **Coin supply** — Place all coins in a supply.
8. **Turn order track** — Place near the board with bid markers.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Per-Player Setup
1. **Camels** — Receive 8 camels in your player color.
2. **Starting coins** — Receive 50 coins (kept hidden from other players).
3. **Turn order marker** — Place 1 marker on the turn order track.
4. **No starting cards** — Players begin with no resource cards or Djinns.

#### Hidden Information
- **Coins are secret** — Players keep their coin totals hidden behind a screen or cupped hand. This is a critical rule.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Round
- **Initial turn order** — Randomly determine the initial turn order for the first round.
- **Bidding** — Starting from the second round onward, players bid coins for turn order.

#### Board State
- Each tile has exactly 3 random meeples on it.
- 9 resource cards are visible in the market row.
- 3 Djinn cards are visible.
- The game is ready to begin.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Bid for turn order** — Each player secretly places coins (0+) to bid on going first. Highest bidder goes first. Ties broken by previous turn order position. Bid coins go to the supply.
2. **Player turns** — In turn order, each player performs a mancala move and actions.
3. **Clean up** — Refill market row to 9 cards and Djinn display to 3 cards.

#### On Your Turn
1. **Pick up meeples** — Choose any tile on the board and pick up ALL meeples on it.
2. **Sow meeples** — Move in orthogonal directions (no diagonals), dropping 1 meeple per tile as you move through adjacent tiles. You must change direction or continue straight, but cannot backtrack to the tile you just left.
3. **Last meeple rule** — The last meeple you drop must match the color of at least 1 other meeple already on that destination tile.
4. **Collect tribe** — Pick up all meeples of the last-dropped color from the final tile (including the one you just placed). These meeples determine your tribe action.
5. **Tile action** — Perform the action of the tile where your last meeple landed.
6. **Claim tile (optional)** — If the final tile is now empty of all meeples, place one of your camels to claim it.

#### Tile Claiming
- Place 1 camel on an empty tile to claim ownership. You score that tile's VP at game end.
- You can place a palm tree or palace on claimed tiles for extra VP.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Five Tribe Actions (based on collected meeple color)
- **Viziers (yellow)** — Keep collected Viziers in front of you. At game end, score 10 VP per Vizier PLUS 10 bonus VP for each opponent with fewer Viziers than you.
- **Elders (white)** — Keep collected Elders in front of you. Each Elder is worth 2 VP at game end. Elders are also spent to invoke (buy) Djinns.
- **Merchants (green)** — Take resource cards from the front of the market row equal to the number of Merchants collected. Resources are sold in sets for coins.
- **Builders (blue)** — Earn coins equal to (number of Builders collected) x (number of surrounding blue-value tiles including the tile you are on). Adjacency counts tiles orthogonally adjacent.
- **Assassins (red)** — Kill (remove from game) 1 meeple on the board within range equal to the number of Assassins collected. OR kill 1 Vizier or Elder from an opponent.

#### Tile Actions
- **Oasis** — Place 1 palm tree on this tile (3 VP each at end).
- **Village** — Place 1 palace on this tile (5 VP each at end).
- **Small Market** — Draw the top 3 resource cards, keep 1, discard the rest.
- **Large Market** — Draw the top 6 resource cards, keep 2, discard the rest.
- **Sacred Place** — Pay 1 Elder (or 1 Elder + 1 other resource) to take 1 Djinn from the display.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Djinns
- **Cost** — Pay 1 Elder + 1 Elder (2 Elders) to take a Djinn from the display at a Sacred Place. Or spend 1 Elder + 1 any meeple through certain abilities.
- **Powers** — Djinns grant unique ongoing powers: extra VP for specific tiles, protection from Assassins, bonus coins, extra movement options, etc.
- **VP value** — Each Djinn card is also worth VP printed on the card (variable) at game end.

#### Merchandise / Resource Selling
- Collected resource cards can be sold at any time for coins based on set size:
  - 1 unique card = 1 coin
  - 2 unique = 3 coins
  - 3 unique = 7 coins
  - 4 unique = 13 coins
  - 5 unique = 21 coins
  - 6 unique = 30 coins
  - 7 unique = 40 coins
  - 8 unique = 50 coins
  - 9 unique = 60 coins

#### Assassin Mechanics
- **Range** — Number of Assassins collected = number of tiles away you can reach to remove a meeple.
- **Remove from game** — The killed meeple is removed from the game permanently.
- **Claim tile** — If killing the last meeple on a tile empties it, you may claim it.
- **Attack opponents** — Alternatively, remove 1 Vizier or Elder from an opponent's collected pile.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Game End Trigger
- The game ends when either: a player places their last camel, or no legal meeple move can be made by the active player.
- Finish the current round so all players who bid have taken a turn.

#### Final Scoring (sum all)
1. **Coins** — 1 VP per coin remaining.
2. **Viziers** — 10 VP per Vizier you own, plus 10 VP for each opponent who has strictly fewer Viziers.
3. **Elders** — 2 VP per Elder you own.
4. **Tiles** — VP as printed on each tile you claimed with a camel.
5. **Palm trees** — 3 VP per palm tree on your claimed tiles.
6. **Palaces** — 5 VP per palace on your claimed tiles.
7. **Djinns** — VP as printed on each Djinn card.
8. **Unsold merchandise** — Sell all remaining resource cards at current set values, converting to coins (then VP).

#### Tiebreaker
- Most Viziers + Elders combined. Then most goods cards.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Game
- **Scout the board** — Look for tiles with large concentrations of same-color meeples, especially Merchants near full markets.
- **Bid wisely** — Going first is powerful but expensive. Early underbidding can save coins for the final scoring.
- **Grab key Djinns** — Some Djinns are game-changing (e.g., those giving VP per tile type). Prioritize Sacred Places early.
- **Builder combos** — Builders next to high-value tile clusters can generate massive coin payouts. Look for blue-heavy areas.
- **Vizier accumulation** — Start collecting Viziers early. The majority bonus scales with opponents.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Overbidding** — Spending too many coins on turn order leaves you VP-poor at the end. Coins = points.
- **Ignoring Vizier majority** — The Vizier bonus (10 VP per opponent with fewer) is enormous. Letting one player monopolize Viziers is dangerous.
- **Forgetting to sell goods** — Unsold goods still convert, but selling at optimal set sizes during the game is more efficient for mid-game purchases.
- **Neglecting tile claims** — Empty tiles give you VP and locations for palms/palaces. Always claim when possible.
- **Illegal moves** — The last meeple MUST match a color already on the destination tile. This is the most commonly forgotten rule.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Critical Choices
- **Bid amount** — Balance going first for the best move vs. keeping coins for final scoring.
- **Meeple pickup selection** — The choice of which tile to pick up from determines everything. Look several moves ahead.
- **Assassin targets** — Killing an opponent's Vizier is often more valuable than killing a board meeple. But clearing a tile to claim it can also be huge.
- **When to sell goods** — Larger sets are exponentially more valuable. Hold goods for bigger sets if you can.
- **Djinn selection** — Choose Djinns that synergize with your developing strategy (tile claiming, Merchant focus, etc.).
